title: What Quarto Can Do
text:
Quarto turns plain .qmd files into interactive websites, PDFs, and presentations — no web development experience needed.
This mini-site explores the sine function and demonstrates Quarto’s capabilities along the way.

title: What Quarto Can Do
text:

title: Quiz
text:
Test your understanding of the sine function — and Quarto’s features, powered by the quarto-quizdown extension (Sauer 2023) by Parmsam.

---
shuffleAnswers: true
shuffleQuestions: false
---

# What is $\sin(0)$?

1. [x] $0$
2. [ ] $1$
3. [ ] $-1$
4. [ ] undefined

# What is the maximum value of $\sin(x)$?

1. [ ] $0$
2. [ ] $\pi$
3. [x] $1$
4. [ ] $2$

# What is the period of $\sin(x)$?

1. [ ] $\pi$
2. [x] $2\pi$
3. [ ] $4\pi$
4. [ ] $\frac{\pi}{2}$

# Which extension makes Python run live in the browser?

1. [x] pyodide
2. [ ] quizdown
3. [ ] jsxgraph
4. [ ] py-exercise

# Which file extension does Quarto use for source files?

1. [ ] `.html`
2. [ ] `.py`
3. [x] `.qmd`
4. [ ] `.tex`





References

Sauer, Patrick. 2023. “Quarto-Quizdown: Quizdown Extension for Quarto.” https://github.com/parmsam/quarto-quizdown.

title: Code
text: Quarto supports three ways to include Python code — all powered by Pyodide (Droettboom et al. 2021), which runs Python directly in the browser.

title: Code
text:
Output Only
The code runs automatically in the background; only the result is shown:


sin(  0°) = 0.0000
sin( 30°) = 0.5000
sin( 45°) = 0.7071
sin( 60°) = 0.8660
sin( 90°) = 1.0000
sin(180°) = 0.0000




The Code for this Output looked like this:

```python
#| execute: true # This makes the Code execute during render
#| echo: false # This makes the Code invisible in the final output 
import math

angles = [0, 30, 45, 60, 90, 180]
for deg in angles:
    val = math.sin(math.radians(deg))   
    print(f"sin({deg:3d}°) = {val:.4f}")
```

text:

Click Run — the code executes live in the browser:

title: Quarto Tutorial
text:
This is a Tutorial for using Quarto in a creating context.
If you never saw a Quarto page before click here! If you want to learn how to create something with Quarto click here! If you already know how to create Quarto Documents and want to become better click here!

title: Layout & Formatting
text: Quarto supports rich layout without writing any HTML or CSS.

title: Layout & Formatting
text:
Callouts






NoteNote



Use notes for background information or definitions.








TipTip



Use tips for best practices or practical advice.








WarningWarning



Use warnings to flag common mistakes.








ImportantImportant



Use important for things students must not miss.

title: Layout & Formatting
text:
Columns


Concept
The sine function maps an angle to the y-coordinate on the unit circle.
\[f: \mathbb{R} \to [-1, 1]\]

Key values



\(x\)
\(0\)
\(\frac{\pi}{6}\)
\(\frac{\pi}{2}\)
\(\pi\)




\(\sin(x)\)
\(0\)
\(0.5\)
\(1\)
\(0\)

title: Layout & Formatting
text:
Tabs

DefinitionFormulaProperties


The sine function is one of the fundamental trigonometric functions. It is periodic with period \(2\pi\) and range \([-1, 1]\).


Using the Taylor series expansion:
\[\sin(x) = \sum_{n=0}^{\infty} \frac{(-1)^n}{(2n+1)!}\, x^{2n+1}
           = x - \frac{x^3}{6} + \frac{x^5}{120} - \cdots\]



Period: \(2\pi\)
Range: \([-1,\, 1]\)
Zeros: \(x = k\pi,\; k \in \mathbb{Z}\)
Maxima: \(x = \frac{\pi}{2} + 2k\pi\)

title: References
text: Quarto supports citations and bibliographies via standard .bib files — no plugins needed.

title: References
text:
How It Works

Create a references.bib file with BibTeX entries.
Add bibliography: references.bib to the page’s YAML frontmatter.
Cite anywhere in the text using [@key].

The bibliography is rendered automatically at the end of the page.






TipExample



Writing [@quarto] in your text produces the inline citation: (Posit Software, PBC 2022)
The full entry appears below under References.

title: References
text:
Tools Used in This Example
All interactive features on this site are powered by open-source tools:

Quarto (Posit Software, PBC 2022) — the publishing system behind this entire site
Pyodide (Droettboom et al. 2021) — runs Python directly in the browser (see Code)
JSXGraph (Miller 2023) — interactive mathematics in the browser (see Visualizations)
quizdown (Sauer 2023) — interactive multiple-choice quizzes in the browser (see Quiz)
